skill.name: pc-games
skill.description: Guidance for planning PC and console games, including engine selection, platform features, input design, and optimization priorities.
skill.summary: Plan PC and console game projects with practical engine, platform, controller, and performance guidance.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged markdown backticks, weak cryptography keywords, and system reconnaissance keywords. Review found these are false positives in a documentation-only skill about game development, with no executable commands, network behavior, data access, or prompt injection evidence.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Code Fences Detected as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The flagged backticks are markdown fences around a decision tree and controller mapping notes. They do not invoke Ruby, shell execution, or any external command.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged text is inside plain markdown documentation blocks. There is no executable Ruby syntax, command substitution, or user-controlled command path.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Game Engine Text Detected as Weak Cryptography
low_findings.description: The flagged lines describe PC and console game development and Unreal Blueprint usage. No cryptographic algorithm, hashing API, or security protocol is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The semantic context is game development documentation. The lines contain no crypto primitives such as MD5, SHA1, DES, or insecure random generation.

low_findings.title: False Positive: GDScript Reference Detected as Reconnaissance
low_findings.description: The flagged line lists GDScript as a Godot language option for rapid iteration. It does not request host information, enumerate files, or inspect system configuration.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line is a normal engine-specific recommendation. No reconnaissance commands, filesystem traversal, environment access, or network collection behavior appears nearby.

content.user_title: Plan PC and Console Game Development
content.value_statement: Game teams often struggle to choose engines, platform features, and optimization priorities early. This skill provides concise guidance for engine selection, controller support, platform integration, and profiling decisions.
actual_capabilities: Compares Unity, Godot, and Unreal for 2D, 3D, team size, cost, and learning curve.
Summarizes Steam features such as achievements, cloud saves, leaderboards, Workshop, and rich presence.
Explains console certification categories for PlayStation, Xbox, and Nintendo releases.
Recommends action-based controller mapping across Xbox, PlayStation, and Nintendo layouts.
Lists profiling tools and common performance bottlenecks for Unity, Godot, and Unreal.
Highlights engine-specific principles for Unity 6, Godot 4, and Unreal 5.
limitations: Does not generate complete game source code or engine project files.
Does not replace official platform certification documentation.
Does not verify current engine pricing, licensing, or platform policy changes.
Does not provide art direction, monetization strategy, or publishing contracts.

use_cases.title: Select an Engine for a New Game
use_cases.description: Compare Unity, Godot, and Unreal against project scope, platform targets, team size, and visual requirements.

use_cases.title: Prepare Platform Feature Requirements
use_cases.description: Identify Steam and console features that should be planned before production milestones.

use_cases.title: Improve Input and Performance Planning
use_cases.description: Design controller abstractions and profiling priorities before implementation creates expensive rework.

prompt_templates.title: Choose a Game Engine
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the pc-games skill to recommend an engine for my game. The game is [2D or 3D], targets [platforms], has a team of [size], and needs [key features].

prompt_templates.title: Plan Platform Features
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the pc-games skill to create a platform feature checklist for a [genre] game launching on [PC storefronts or consoles]. Include Steam features and console certification concerns.

prompt_templates.title: Design Controller Support
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the pc-games skill to review my controller plan for [game type]. Recommend action mappings, haptic feedback levels, and cross-platform input risks.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Performance Strategy
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the pc-games skill to evaluate the performance plan for my [Unity, Godot, or Unreal] game. Focus on profiling tools, bottlenecks, asset strategy, and engine-specific optimization choices.

output_examples.input: I am making a 2D pixel-art action game for PC with a small team.
output_examples.output: Recommended direction: consider Godot 4 for strong 2D support and fast iteration. Plan Steam achievements, cloud saves, and action-based controller mappings early.

output_examples.input: I need a controller support review for a console action game.
output_examples.output: Expected guidance: map player actions instead of buttons, account for Xbox, PlayStation, and Nintendo confirm and cancel conventions, and reserve stronger haptics for major events.

output_examples.input: Our Unreal 5 game has performance concerns before launch.
output_examples.output: Expected guidance: profile first with Unreal Insights, review draw calls and shader cost, use Nanite where appropriate, and avoid optimization before evidence.

best_practices: Start with project needs, target platforms, team skills, and content scale before choosing an engine.
Plan platform features and certification requirements before release milestones.
Profile early and use engine-specific tools before changing systems for performance.
anti_patterns: Choosing an engine because it is popular instead of matching project requirements.
Hardcoding controller buttons instead of mapping player actions.
Skipping profiling and guessing at performance bottlenecks.

faq.question: Which engines does this skill compare?
faq.answer: It compares Unity 6, Godot 4, and Unreal 5 for common PC and console game development decisions.

faq.question: Can this skill help with Steam features?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers achievements, cloud saves, leaderboards, Workshop, and rich presence at a planning level.

faq.question: Does this skill include console certification rules?
faq.answer: It names the main certification categories, but teams still need official PlayStation, Xbox, and Nintendo documentation.

faq.question: Can it write production game code?
faq.answer: No. It provides planning principles and review guidance, not complete implementation code.

faq.question: How should I use it for controller support?
faq.answer: Ask it to review action mappings across Xbox, PlayStation, and Nintendo conventions before implementing input code.

faq.question: Is the security audit safe for publication?
faq.answer: Yes. The reviewed findings were false positives in markdown documentation, and no executable risky behavior was found.
